title: allium subhirsutum var. album
intro: Allium subhirsutum var. album, also known as the white desert garlic, is a type of plant that belongs to the Allium genus, a group of plants commonly known as onions and garlic. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, antifungal, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Allium subhirsutum var. album include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The herbal preparations of this plant include using its leaves and bulbs in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
